methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Nox Metals is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Zane Hengsperger that has raised $5.1M to date. The valuation universe comprises nine private peers (including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity) and one public comparable (277810.KQ), though peers span varying stages and valuations within the robotics sector.

The stage-progression method is the applicable valuation approach here. Using total capital raised ($5.1M) as a baseline value proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression in this sector yields an implied valuation of $13M. A peer-median approach could not be applied—while seven robotics and manufacturing peers exist in the broader database, zero same-stage comparables, zero adjacent-stage comparables, and zero similar-tier comparables met the minimum threshold of three datapoints required for reliable triangulation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $11M to $15M, centered at $13M. This single-method conclusion carries meaningful uncertainty given the reliance on stage progression alone and the absence of narrow peer validation. Key unknowns include Nox Metals' revenue traction, burn rate, technology differentiation within robotics, and path to Series A. Practitioners should treat this range as directional pending additional comparable data or operational milestones.
